content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as recursive self-improving agents: the memory system itself acts as an independent curiosity-driven agent that selects which new experiences the base active learning agent should encode, rather than acting as a passive storage layer. This recursive agent architecture lets the memory system proactively prune low-value outdated memories and query for missing information that reduces the base agent’s generalization error, aligning both layers’ learning objectives.
